title: Platanthera michaelii
taxonomy: Platanthera michaelii is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It is a member of the genus Platanthera, which is a group of orchids commonly known as bog orchids. Within the genus Platanthera, Platanthera michaelii is classified as a terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows on land rather than in water or on other plants. The species Platanthera michaelii is native to North America and is known for its distinctive white flowers with a yellow or greenish-yellow lip. It is a relatively rare and endangered species due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.
uses: Platanthera michaelii, also known as Michael's orchid, has been traditionally used in folk medicine, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
